evals.prompt: Research the codebase using the questions artifact at research/questions/2026-06-01-csv-export.html. The artifact's Context section describes a CSV export feature ticket; its Questions section asks how exports, downloads, and column-visibility settings currently work.
evals.expected_output: A research document at research/docs/YYYY-MM-DD-topic.html synthesized from parallel sub-agent findings, containing only facts about the current codebase with file:line references, with the ticket context never forwarded to sub-agents
expectations: Reads the questions artifact fully in the main context, but sub-agent prompts contain ONLY questions and scope — never the ticket, feature description, or phrasing like 'we are adding CSV export'
Inherits crispy:breaking_changes_allowed from the questions artifact instead of re-asking the user, and threads it into every sub-agent prompt
Spawns parallel sub-agents (codebase-locator, codebase-analyzer, codebase-pattern-finder, research-locator/analyzer as relevant) rather than doing deep file reading in the main context
Waits for ALL sub-agents before synthesizing; findings cite concrete file:line references
The document contains no recommendations or evaluative language — current state only (documentarian, not evaluator)
Emits the full crispy:* metadata set including breaking_changes_allowed and compatibility_context, with matching visible Summary rows
Body sections appear in order: research question, compatibility context, summary, detailed findings, code references, architecture & conventions, historical context, related research
Presents an executive summary, offers to commit the artifact via gh-commit, and asks for follow-up questions

evals.prompt: Research this inline query with --format=md: 'I'm building a notification center feature — find out how our SignalR hubs are registered and how clients subscribe to them.'
evals.expected_output: A Markdown research document where the inline query was rephrased into pure current-state questions before any sub-agent was spawned, with the feature intent stripped
expectations: Rephrases the query into intent-free questions (e.g. 'how are SignalR hubs registered and how do clients subscribe') — the phrase 'building a notification center' never reaches a sub-agent
Because no questions artifact exists, determines the compatibility posture itself: infers from the request or asks the user once via AskUserQuestion before decomposing
Because --format=md was passed, delegates to Skill('output-markdown') and writes research/docs/YYYY-MM-DD-<topic>.md with crispy:* keys in YAML frontmatter
Runs fresh codebase research via sub-agents rather than relying solely on existing research documents
Handles follow-up questions by appending to the same document and updating crispy:last_updated metadata in sync with the visible Summary cells
Writes no artifacts outside the research/ directory
